Avoid milk, antacids, iron, calcium, magnesium within 2‚Äď3 hours.

medicines.medicineAdvantage:

medicines.medicineAdvantage:
ūüĒĻHigh Selectivity for Serotonin Transporter
‚ě•The most selective SSRI, meaning it acts almost purely on serotonin with minimal effect on dopamine, norepinephrine, or other receptors. This leads to fewer side effects than many antidepressants.
ūüĒĻBetter Tolerability
‚ě•Compared with many SSRIs and SNRIs, causes less nausea, agitation, and drug interactions, so many physicians prefer it as first-line therapy for depression and anxiety.
ūüĒĻEffective for Both Depression and Anxiety
‚ě•One medication can treat depressive disorder, anxiety disorder, panic disorder, and social anxiety, making it useful when diagnosis overlaps.
ūüĒĻMinimal Drug Interactions
‚ě•Compared with other SSRIs (especially less than fluoxetine and paroxetine).
ūüĒĻFaster Onset Among SSRIs
‚ě•Some studies show symptom improvement within 1‚Äď2 weeks, slightly earlier than several other SSRIs.
medicines.medicineDisadvantage:
ūüĒłSexual Dysfunction
‚ě•Common SSRI problem including decreased libido, delayed ejaculation, and anorgasmia, leading to treatment discontinuation in some patients.
ūüĒłQT Interval Prolongation
‚ě•At higher doses (especially >20 mg/day) can prolong QT interval, increasing risk of arrhythmia in susceptible patients.
ūüĒłInitial Anxiety or Insomnia
‚ě•Some patients develop temporary restlessness, insomnia, or anxiety during the first weeks.
ūüĒłWithdrawal Symptoms if Abruptly Stopped
‚ě•Stopping suddenly may cause dizziness, irritability, sensory disturbances, and flu-like symptoms.
ūüĒłDelayed Full Therapeutic Effect
‚ě•Full antidepressant benefit usually appears after 4‚Äď6 weeks, which may frustrate some patients.
